plan_adherence_notes: All 4 plan steps executed successfully. Step 1 (ResolveMarketPanel): 176 lines, 12 tests covering guard clauses (resolved summary, draft returns null, no accountId returns null), outcome selection, two-step confirmation flow, cancel, outcome-change reset, mutation success/error. Also correctly tests closed-market rendering. Step 2 (ConditionalEditor): 252 lines, 11 tests covering unconfigured session placeholder, UI elements, add/remove context rows, disabled context outcome select before market selection, market-to-outcome cascade, unconditional/conditional button text, payload structure assertion, success/error. Step 3 (VariableRow): 277 lines, 14 tests covering null return when data not loaded, market title and probability bars, assume/unassume toggling, edit button visibility (with/without session), inline edit slider/cancel/set, mutation payload, contextPayload from AssumptionContext, success/error, target market '(current)' label, no assume button for target. Step 4: all 37 tests pass. Minor deviation: mock approach uses simpler vi.mock('@/lib/api/client') without vi.importActual (existing EventTradePanel.test.tsx uses importActual), but functionally correct. Line counts slightly exceeded estimates (580 estimated vs 705 actual) due to thorough coverage.
missed_questions: Questions q1-q3 were based on the false premise that test files already existed. A foundational question like 'Do these test files exist yet?' should have been asked first.
Should have asked what specific API client function names (resolveMarket, submitProbabilityEdit, listMarkets, getMarket) need to be mocked, rather than referencing hook names.
Should have asked about the useMarkets response shape (marketsListResponse with markets array, count, meta) needed for ConditionalEditor's otherMarkets filtering.
reason: All 37 tests pass across 3 test files. Implementation follows the plan with good fidelity: all planned scenarios are covered (guard clauses, user interactions, mutation flows, error/success states), the AssumptionProvider wrapper approach works correctly, and ContextRow disabled-state testing (from research q4) is included. Tests follow project conventions (vi.mock, renderWithProviders, localStorage session seeding, waitFor async). The question phase had a significant flaw (assumed test files existed), but research corrected this completely and the implementation proceeded on a solid foundation. No production code was modified. Test coverage is thorough and tests are well-structured.
